title: Norms Application Matrix by Canonical Location

selective_application_logic.description: V1/V2/V3 apply ONLY when artifact explicitly handles vector dimensions or pgvector operators. Never apply to non-vector artifacts.
trigger_conditions: file content contains pgvector operators (<->, <#>, <=>) WITH explicit vector(n) dimension declaration
schema includes embedding dimension validation (e.g., vector(1536), hnsw, ivfflat)
exclusion_conditions: artifact_type in ['skill_yaml', 'skill_json_schema', 'skill_sql', 'skill_go'] AND canonical_path NOT contains 'postgres-pgvector/'
file content mentions 'vector' only as generic term (e.g., 'feature vector', 'state vector') without pgvector context
LANGUAGE LOCK violation detected: pgvector operators in yaml-json-schema/, sql/, go/ folders

validation_behavior.if_triggers_met: Apply V1/V2/V3 with same intensity rules as C-constraints (mandatory/applicable/contextual)
validation_behavior.if_exclusions_met: Treat V1/V2/V3 as 'not_applicable' - omit from validation report entirely, do not check
validation_behavior.if_ambiguous: Default to exclusion + emit warning for human review in orchestrator log

V1.name: Vector Dimension Declaration
V1.description: Explicit declaration of embedding dimensions (e.g., vector(1536), vector(768)) with validation of supported sizes
checks: explicit vector(dim) declaration in CREATE TABLE or column definition

V2.name: Distance Metric Explicit
V2.description: Validation of pgvector distance operator (<->, <#>, <=>) alignment with index opclass and documentation
checks: operator documented in comment (e.g., '-- <=> = cosine distance')
operator matches index opclass (l2_ops↔<->, cosine_ops↔<=>, ip_ops↔<#>)
no ambiguous ORDER BY vec without explicit operator

V3.name: Index-Type Match Justified
V3.description: Ensures pgvector index type (hnsw/ivfflat) and parameters are justified by dataset size and query pattern
checks: index type (hnsw/ivfflat) justified by comment (e.g., 'hnsw for <100k vectors')
parameters explicit (m=16, ef_construction=100 for hnsw; lists=100 for ivfflat)
CONCURRENTLY used for production index creation
tenant_id included in WHERE clause of vector queries

master_precedence_rule.description: Rule of precedence for norm application based on canonical location + selective vector logic

logic.condition: file_path contains '06-PROGRAMMING/postgres-pgvector/' AND content matches pgvector operators
logic.action: Apply C1-C8 COMPLETE + V1-V3 SELECTIVE (if trigger conditions met)
Apply C1-C8 COMPLETE (Tier 2-3 target); V* excluded unless explicit vector schema
Apply C1-C8 COMPLETE; HARD BLOCK on V* application + pgvector operators (LANGUAGE LOCK)

logic.condition: file_path contains '02-SKILLS/BASE DE DATOS-RAG/' AND inline SQL uses pgvector
logic.action: C4 MANDATORY + V1-V3 CONTEXTUAL (only if embedding dimensions declared)
C1-C8 minimum validation; V* explicitly excluded

mandatory.behavior: Automatic failure if not met. Blocks tier certification. Exit code 2.

applicable.behavior: Must be present and verifiable. Warning for Tier 2-3 if missing, allows Tier 1 with correction.

contextual.behavior: Applies only if file function requires it. Checked conditionally based on inline content analysis.

not_applicable.behavior: Norm irrelevant for this file type. Omitted from validation report entirely. V* ALWAYS not_applicable unless trigger conditions met.

02-SKILLS/AI/.description: AI model integration patterns (OpenRouter, Qwen, etc.)

02-SKILLS/INFRAESTRUCTURA/.description: Infrastructure patterns: Docker, SSH, UFW, monitoring

02-SKILLS/BASE DE DATOS-RAG/.description: Database schemas, RAG ingestion, multi-tenant queries

checks: tenant_id as second field in tables

V1.condition: if inline SQL declares vector(dim) columns

02-SKILLS/WHATSAPP-RAG AGENTS/.description: WhatsApp agents with RAG + multi-tenant awareness

V1.condition: if service uses pgvector image or declares VECTOR_DIM env var

checks: validation prohibiting localhost in inference endpoints

05-CONFIGURATIONS/pipelines/promptfoo/.description: Prompt evaluation configuration and test cases

V3.condition: if script validates vector index safety
if script validates vector index safety

06-PROGRAMMING/sql/.description: Pure PostgreSQL patterns - LANGUAGE LOCK: NO pgvector operators

V1.reason: LANGUAGE LOCK: sql/ handles pure PostgreSQL, no pgvector extension

anti_drift_checks: examples must be pure PostgreSQL 14+ syntax

06-PROGRAMMING/postgres-pgvector/.description: pgvector-specific patterns: indexing, querying, migration - ONLY location for vector operators

06-PROGRAMMING/yaml-json-schema/.description: YAML/JSON Schema validation patterns - LANGUAGE LOCK: NO pgvector operators

checks: required fields include tenant_id where applicable

V1.reason: LANGUAGE LOCK: yaml-json-schema/ handles config schemas, not vector dimensions

anti_drift_checks: examples must be pure YAML/JSON, zero SQL

06-PROGRAMMING/go/.description: Go services for MCP - LANGUAGE LOCK: NO SQL/pgvector operators

V1.reason: LANGUAGE LOCK: go/ handles MCP services, not vector DB operations

tier_requirements.note: Procedures prioritize clarity over technical exhaustiveness

V1.condition: if log schema includes embedding fields

09-TEST-SANDBOX/.description: Isolated testing space for multi-IA generation comparison

tier_requirements.note: Tier max is 1; promotion to production requires explicit re-certification

*.promotion_rule: Files in sandbox cannot be merged to production without explicit promotion workflow and re-validation against target folder matrix

rules.condition: file_path contains 'BASE DE DATOS-RAG' OR 'INFRAESTRUCTURA' OR 'VERTICALES'
actions: C4 (tenant_id) MANDATORY in all inline code examples
C3 (secrets) applies to any credential placeholder
V1/V3 MANDATORY if pgvector operators present
V2 APPLICABLE for distance metric documentation
C4 applies only if file mentions queries or payloads
V* explicitly excluded unless trigger conditions met

.yaml.description: Decision rules for YAML files (Docker, Promptfoo, CI/CD)
rules: C1/C2: mem_limit, cpus, pids_limit, timeout declared explicitly
C3: ZERO hardcode; use ${VAR:?missing} or env_file references
C4: tenant_id in labels, networks, volumes, environment
C7: healthcheck + restart policy on critical services
C8: logging driver json-file + structured context
V1: contextual only if VECTOR_DIM env var declared with validation
Validator: yq eval + docker compose config --quiet (if applicable)
C1/C2: validation {} blocks for resource ranges
C3: sensitive = true on secret outputs; default = null + TF_VAR_*
C4: tenant_id as required variable with regex validation
C5: checksums in backup/artifact outputs
C6: validation prohibiting localhost in inference endpoints
V1: contextual only if module declares vector-related resources
Validator: terraform fmt -check + terraform validate -no-color -json
Frontmatter COMMENTED with # (for bash compatibility)
Shebang #!/bin/bash + set -euo pipefail mandatory
C3: ${VAR:?missing} in all credentials
C4: tenant_id injected in logs, paths, queries
C7: timeout in curl/wget, retry with backoff if applicable
C8: JSON output via heredoc (cat <<EOF)
V*: contextual only if script name contains 'vector' or calls vector validators
Validator: shellcheck + bash -n + audit-secrets.sh

.sql.description: Decision rules for SQL files - LANGUAGE LOCK enforcement
rules: If in sql/: PROHIBIT vector(n), <->, <#>, <=>, hnsw, ivfflat
If in postgres-pgvector/: REQUIRE V1/V3 mandatory, V2 applicable
C4: tenant_id in all WHERE clauses (RLS defense in depth)
C1: LIMIT + resource hints on all SELECT queries
Validator: check-rls.sh + verify-vector-dims.sh (if pgvector path)

ai_generation_prompt_checklist.description: Mandatory checklist to include in ALL AI generation prompts
items: Before generating, read PROJECT_TREE.md and resolve:
1. Exact canonical path of file to create
2. Extension → apply validation rules by type (see C1-C8 + V* matrix)
3. Function → determine target tier (1/2/3) and required examples (5/10/25)
4. Dominant constraints → C4 mandatory if path contains 'DB-RAG', 'INFRA', 'VERTICAL'
5. Vector constraints → V1/V3 mandatory ONLY if path is postgres-pgvector/ AND uses pgvector operators
6. LANGUAGE LOCK → NEVER use vector(n), <->, hnsw in sql/, yaml-json-schema/, go/
7. Frontmatter → commented if .sh, pure YAML if .md/.yaml/.tf/.json
8. Zero hardcode → use ${VAR:?missing} or sensitive = true
9. tenant_id → present in queries, labels, logs, payloads if applicable
11. Validation command → executable and specific to this file type
12. If context missing: '⚠️ BLOCKED: missing [X]. Awaiting context.' and STOP.

orchestrator_integration_notes.matrix_lookup_strategy: Use longest prefix match on file_path to find most specific matrix entry
orchestrator_integration_notes.vector_constraint_resolution: Before applying V1/V2/V3, evaluate selective_application_logic.trigger_conditions AND exclusion_conditions. If excluded, treat as not_applicable regardless of content.
orchestrator_integration_notes.language_lock_enforcement: If file_path contains 'yaml-json-schema/' OR 'sql/' OR 'go/' AND content matches pgvector operators (<->, <#>, <=>, vector(n), hnsw, ivfflat), ABORT with exit code 2 and message 'LANGUAGE LOCK VIOLATION: pgvector operators not permitted in <folder>'
orchestrator_integration_notes.validator_execution: Only execute validators marked as applicable/mandatory for the resolved profile. Skip vector validators if V* excluded by LANGUAGE LOCK or trigger conditions.
orchestrator_integration_notes.tier_calculation: Calculate achieved tier based on passed C-constraints + (V-constraints if applicable) + examples count + functional requirements
orchestrator_integration_notes.blocking_behavior: If any mandatory check fails (C or V), return exit code 2 immediately without tier calculation

json_output_schema.vector_constraints_applied: boolean (true only if selective_application_logic triggers met)

validated_examples: ✅ Should see: '06-PROGRAMMING/postgres-pgvector/vector-indexing.md' → applies C1-C8 + V1-V3 (trigger: pgvector path + operators)
❌ If you see: V1 applied to '06-PROGRAMMING/yaml-json-schema/schema-validation.md' → 🔧 Fix: Enforce hard_block on V* for yaml-json-schema/
✅ Should see: '02-SKILLS/BASE DE DATOS-RAG/rag-ingestion.md' with inline SQL using vector(1536) → V1 contextual, V3 contextual
❌ If you see: V3 blocking on file with 'vector' as generic term (no pgvector operators) → 🔧 Fix: Improve trigger condition to require explicit pgvector context
✅ Should see: docker-compose with VECTOR_DIM=1536 → V1 contextual applies, validates dimension value
❌ If you see: orchestrator applies V2 to validation script that doesn't handle indexes → 🔧 Fix: Check self-check condition in vector_constraints
✅ Should see: LANGUAGE LOCK violation (pgvector in yaml-json-schema/) → exit 2 with clear error message
❌ If you see: constraints_mapped in frontmatter includes V1 for artifact_type: skill_yaml → 🔧 Fix: validate-frontmatter.sh must enforce selective logic
✅ Should see: tier_requirements for postgres-pgvector/ includes v1_v3_blocking: true
✅ Should see: json output includes vector_constraints_applied: false for non-vector artifacts
✅ Should see: sql/ files with vector(1536) → ABORT with LANGUAGE LOCK violation
✅ Should see: BASE DE DATOS-RAG/ with pure SQL (no pgvector) → V* not_applicable, only C1-C8

changes: Added vector_constraints_mapped: [V1, V2, V3] at root level
Implemented selective_application_logic with trigger/exclusion conditions for vector constraints
Added vector_constraints_definitions with validator specs, blocking rules, and checks
Updated matrix_by_location entries for postgres-pgvector/, yaml-json-schema/, sql/, BASE DE DATOS-RAG/
Added LANGUAGE LOCK enforcement: hard_block on V* for non-pgvector folders
Enhanced orchestrator_integration_notes with vector constraint resolution and language_lock_enforcement
Updated json_output_schema to include vector_constraints_applied and language_lock_status fields
Added validated_examples for selective V* application and LANGUAGE LOCK scenarios
Updated extension_decision_rules to include SQL language lock and vector trigger conditions
Enhanced ai_generation_prompt_checklist with vector constraint and LANGUAGE LOCK guidance
